name: Synchrony Bank
description_short: Synchrony is the largest private-label credit card provider in the US, partnering with Amazon, Lowe's, PayPal, and others. Also offers high-yield savings, CDs, and personal loans. NYSE: SYF, FDIC-insured.
description_long:
Synchrony Bank is a community bank located in Draper, UT, established in 1988. The bank holds approximately $112115M in total assets and $83376M in deposits. It operates 2 office locations. Designated as a NONE. FDIC insured — deposits protected up to $250,000.

As a major financial institution, this bank competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans.
pros: FDIC insured — deposits protected up to $250,000
Local community bank with personal service
cons: May have fewer digital/mobile banking features than national banks
Fewer ATM locations than large banks

certifications: Largest US private-label credit card issuer
Spun off from GE Capital in 2014-2015
CFPB: $259M+ consumer redress (consent order terminated May 2025)

diagnosis: Synchrony is the largest private-label credit card issuer in the US, powering store cards for Amazon, Lowe's, PayPal, Verizon, and dozens of other retailers. With $112B+ in assets and NYSE listing (SYF), it has massive institutional scale. The key concern: a CFPB consent order resulted in $259M+ in consumer redress for discriminatory and deceptive credit card practices (terminated May 2025 after Synchrony fulfilled all obligations). The company also paid $3.5M to California for harassing debt collection calls. Best suited for consumers who want retail financing through their favorite stores, not for those seeking a primary banking relationship.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
